subject: Add support for multiple k8s clusters
commit_message:
Add support for multiple k8s clusters

Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-1136
Change-Id: I2c2f440bf9793b7398d72c5fb97bd9b3b1309416
Signed-off-by: Jack Lucas <jflucas@research.att.com>

Patch Set 1:

Is this merge being held up by the ONAP JobDeployer job failures?  Looks like most of the problems have nothing to do with DCAE (nor with this set of changes).   The DCAE policy handler failure is undoubtedly due to the failures with the policy engine.   I'm not sure about the DCAE deployment handler--would need to see more details.
I don't think it's reasonable to hold this up any longer, given that it's been over two weeks, we're rapidly approaching the code freeze (and have more OOM changes to make before then), and given that we've provided evidence of a successful deployment.

Patch Set 1:

Hello Jack,
I'm don't know why it's not merged. JobDeployer is triggered automatically and usually Morgan or I give a feedback on the result.
Here, you were unlucky as our OpenStack platform had severe outages last weeks so we had to retrigger your review several times, apologies for that.

Now the result is there, and I cannot put a "+1" as two failing pods (onap-dcaegen2-dcae-deployment-handler-64b59c864d-df6v4 and onap-dcaegen2-dcae-policy-handler-6446f84665-wvcnh) are in failing states but are also part of what has been touched by the patch.

It's certainly not the fault of the patch but I cannot be sure so I score "0".

As we (Morgan and I) don't have "+2" or "merge" power, it's not very important.

I also added James MacNider who also has +2/Merge power, so one of the four that can answer may give you a better response on why it's not merged
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully merged by Mike Elliott
